args.query: SELECT gmap_id, name, description FROM business_description WHERE name ILIKE '%massage%' OR description ILIKE '%massage%' OR name ILIKE '%spa%' OR description ILIKE '%spa%' OR name ILIKE '%bodywork%' OR description ILIKE '%bodywork%';

exec_args.sql: SELECT gmap_id, name, description FROM business_description WHERE name ILIKE '%massage%' OR description ILIKE '%massage%' OR name ILIKE '%spa%' OR description ILIKE '%spa%' OR name ILIKE '%bodywork%' OR description ILIKE '%bodywork%';

args.code:
import json, pandas as pd

biz = pd.DataFrame(var_call_qzkjCcApW0oNcz3AGyOeQUVw)

# load full reviews from file path
path = var_call_UeGOj3LvvahwAvAE6ZaXstoW
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)
rev = pd.DataFrame(reviews)
rev['rating'] = pd.to_numeric(rev['rating'], errors='coerce')

avg = rev.groupby('gmap_id', as_index=False)['rating'].mean().rename(columns={'rating':'avg_rating'})

# massage therapy businesses: use name/description keywords
mask = (
    biz['name'].str.contains('massage', case=False, na=False) |
    biz['description'].str.contains('massage', case=False, na=False) |
    biz['name'].str.contains('spa', case=False, na=False) |
    biz['description'].str.contains('spa', case=False, na=False) |
    biz['name'].str.contains('bodywork', case=False, na=False) |
    biz['description'].str.contains('bodywork', case=False, na=False)
)
biz2 = biz[mask].copy()

out = biz2.merge(avg, on='gmap_id', how='left')
out = out.dropna(subset=['avg_rating'])
out = out[out['avg_rating'] >= 4.0]
out = out.sort_values(['avg_rating','name'], ascending=[False, True])

res = out[['name','avg_rating']].to_dict('records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(res))

import json, pandas as pd

biz = pd.DataFrame(var_call_qzkjCcApW0oNcz3AGyOeQUVw)

# load full reviews from file path
path = var_call_UeGOj3LvvahwAvAE6ZaXstoW
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)
rev = pd.DataFrame(reviews)
rev['rating'] = pd.to_numeric(rev['rating'], errors='coerce')

avg = rev.groupby('gmap_id', as_index=False)['rating'].mean().rename(columns={'rating':'avg_rating'})

# massage therapy businesses: use name/description keywords
mask = (
    biz['name'].str.contains('massage', case=False, na=False) |
    biz['description'].str.contains('massage', case=False, na=False) |
    biz['name'].str.contains('spa', case=False, na=False) |
    biz['description'].str.contains('spa', case=False, na=False) |
    biz['name'].str.contains('bodywork', case=False, na=False) |
    biz['description'].str.contains('bodywork', case=False, na=False)
)
biz2 = biz[mask].copy()

out = biz2.merge(avg, on='gmap_id', how='left')
out = out.dropna(subset=['avg_rating'])
out = out[out['avg_rating'] >= 4.0]
out = out.sort_values(['avg_rating','name'], ascending=[False, True])

res = out[['name','avg_rating']].to_dict('records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(res))
